reasoning: The development of a new vaccine typically takes many years and requires extensive clinical trials. My search of the literature up to December 2025 confirms that there are no vaccines for Baylisascaris infection (raccoon roundworm) in any stage of clinical development. The disease in humans is extremely rare, offering almost no commercial incentive for a vaccine to be prioritized. Given that the resolution dates are at most three months away, the probability of a vaccine suddenly being developed, successfully tested, and accurately updated on the specific Wikipedia page is effectively zero. The only realistic chance of a 'Yes' resolution would be due to temporary Wikipedia vandalism on the exact resolution dates, which is highly improbable on such an obscure page. To reflect the required increasing uncertainty over time, I am using slightly increased probabilities for the further dates, while keeping all of them near the minimum possible value.
